overview.short: Mrugagad fort is a hill fort in Sudhagad taluka of Raigad district, Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Mrugagad fort is a hill fort in Sudhagad taluka of Raigad district, Maharashtra, India. At a height of 1750 ft, the fort lies near Bheliv village, and is made of stone. The fort is very small in size and is in a form of spur radiating from the Western Ghats. The fort is very close to the Lonavala, Khandala and Khopoli.

topPlaces.name: Bebad Ohol
topPlaces.description: Bebad Ohol is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 656.97 ha (1,623 acres).

topPlaces.description: Kurvande is a village in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 2,407 ha (5,948 acres).

topPlaces.name: Bhushi Dam
topPlaces.description: The Bhushi Dam is a masonry dam on the Indrayani River in Lonavala, Maharashtra, India. In 2014, the Indian Railways announced plans to develop Bhushi Dam as a tourist resort with participation from the private sector.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mrugagad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mrugagad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (60 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (57 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mrugagad?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mrugagad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mrugagad?
faq.a: Stays in Mrugagad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mrugagad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bebad Ohol, Gevhande Apati, Atvan, Kurvande, Bhushi Dam.
